title: Assessment for Substance Use Disorders - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Assessment for Substance Use Disorders 85 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter At Risk Families Domestic Violence Issues Hispanic/Latino Community Native American Community Veterans Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness BENTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH HEALTH SERVICE BUILDING SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER TREATMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers individual and group counseling for people who want to change their relationship to substance use. Services may also include drug and alcohol assessments, drug screening, and case management. Peer support and recovery mentors are available, along with referrals to mental health and community resources. Also supports parent–child reunification, provides Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT), helps with admissions to detox and residential treatment, and creates relapse prevention plans to support long-term recovery. 530 NW 27th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday -Friday 8am-5pm (541) 766-6767 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BESTCARE TREATMENT SERVICES Services include: individual, family, and group counseling, structured outpatient treatment, and DUII court mandated rehabilitation. 2301 Cove Avenue La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 962-8800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL CITY CONCERN OLD TOWN CLINIC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Old Town Clinic offers a patient-centered primary care. Services include: care for acute and chronic illnesses, women's health services, such as PAPS, pelvic exams, and breast exams, behavioral healthcare evaluation, substance abuse assessment, naturopathic and acupuncture services. Also offers a variety of wellness programs and activities available to patients, such as yoga. Urgent care for established patients. The pharmacy provides medication education and therapy management, as well as anticoagulation therapy management to current clients. 726 W Burnside Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Clinic hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-5pm, Wednesday 9am-5pm Pharmacy hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8:30am-5:30, Wednesday 9:30am-5:30pm Email (503) 228-4533 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DIVISION ALCOHOL AND DRUG TREATMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers alcohol and drug treatment services through a network of providers. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-3pm Email (541) 676-9161 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON ALCOHOLISM FOUNDATION SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides residential and outpatient treatment for adults with drug and/or alcohol addictions. Provides evaluations for people arrested for DUIs, Minor in Possession (MIP) information classes, two transitional living facilities (one for men, one for women and children), and alcohol- and drug-free apartments for those who have successfully completed treatment. The residential program includes a child development program for men, women, and their children. 216 SW Hailey Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who meet criteria for residential alcohol and drug addiction treatment. Mothers and single fathers referred by DHS Child Welfare may have children approved to reside with them. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 278-2558 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EUGENE RELIEF NURSERY ACCESSING SUCCESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for individuals struggling with substance use or those in recovery. Specializes in treatment for those addicted to opioids and alcohol by offering medication to assist in the recovery process. The Deaf and hard of hearing substance use treatment program is one of only a handful in the country. 1601 E Fourth Plain Boulevard Suite A212 Vancouver, WA 98663 Eligibility: Unrestricted, some outpatient programs are available for the Deaf or hard of hearing Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Detox: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Assessments: Monday-Thursday noon-3pm MAR Rapid Response: Wednesday 9am-11am Email (360) 397-8246 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFESTANCE HEALTH LIFESTANCE HEALTH PORTLAND NE MLK CONEXIONES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers culturally and linguistically specific psychological evaluations and counseling. Other services include domestic violence intervention services, moral reconation groups, anger management, dual diagnosis treatment, and urinalysis and other screening tools. Substance use disorder prevention education available for children, parents, and community members. Also provides gambling assessment and treatment. 702 Sunset Drive Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Assessment walk-in hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-3pm Office: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm, Friday 8am-5pm, evening services available Email (541) 889-9167 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN COUNTY ALCOHOL AND DRUG PREVENTION TREATMENT OUTPATIENT ALCOHOL, DRUG, AND GAMBLING TREATMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides prevention and treatment services for alcohol, drug, and gambling addiction.
